seed_problem: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.
mutation: output: replace the unit-cardinality edge budget (remove at most k links) with a weighted CLOSURE-COST budget (each doorway has a closure cost; total closed cost must stay <= K). This turns the interdiction budget into a knapsack-style constraint, so the choice space couples 'which doorway lengthens the tour most' with 'how much budget it consumes', keeping the exact optimum NP-hard while preserving the connectivity validity gate and the delay-ratio scoring.
title: Museum Gallery Tour: Budgeted Doorway Closures
strategies: do-nothing baseline (close no doorways; scores the calibration point)
cheapest-first path interdiction: close the cheapest affordable doorway on the current shortest tour, repeat
best-improvement path interdiction: close the shortest-tour doorway that lengthens the tour the most per step
benefit/cost knapsack greedy or local search over closure subsets that respects the cost budget
